subject: Computer Networking
title: ARPANET's First Message
description: Learn ARPANET's First Message in one minute.

hook.text: On October 29, 1969, a student at UCLA typed 'LOGIN' to a computer at Stanford. The system crashed after 'LO'.

buildup.text: That two-letter transmission was the first message ever sent over ARPANET — the internet's ancestor.

discovery.text: ARPANET initially connected just four universities: UCLA, Stanford, UC Santa Barbara, and the University of Utah.

twist.text: The crash was a bug, not a design flaw. They fixed it and successfully logged in an hour later.

climax.text: From four nodes in 1969, ARPANET grew to dozens by the mid-1970s, connecting research labs across the country.

punchline.text: The internet's first word was 'LO.' Even legends have humble starts.

quiz.question: What was the first message sent over ARPANET?
